articles.title: As a harsh summer looms, Delhi govt. yet to announce heat action plan - The Hindu
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Delhi
articles.full_text:
Ahead of what is projected to be one of the hottest summers in recent years, the Delhi government has yet to declare a heat action plan, which outlines targeted measures to mitigate the impact of extreme heat and humidity, especially on vulnerable populations.
On March 7, Delhi recorded a maximum temperature of 35.7°C — the highest in the first week of the month in the past 50 years — according to data from the India Meteorological Department (IMD).
Presently, relatively mild conditions prevail, with the maximum temperature on Sunday settling at 29.5°C, two notches below the seasonal average, and the minimum at 15.6°C, 1.1 notches below normal, sparing the Capital the early onset of harsh summer heat.
However, this spell of pleasant weather is unlikely to last. On February 28, the IMD indicated that temperatures are expected to rise steadily, with “above-normal maximum temperatures” likely over most parts of north-west India, including Delhi, during the peak summer months.
Last year’s plan, prepared by the Delhi Disaster Management Authority in partnership with the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA), had noted that this year’s action plan should be finalised before the onset of summer. It also identified March to July as the “heat season”.
An official familiar with the developments said this year’s plan is expected to take more time as the government is yet to hold a meeting with the various departments concerned on the issue.
“The NDMA took up last year’s plan for Delhi as a case study, and it was successful in terms of learnings. It has also helped to put structures in place in Delhi,” the Central government official said.
Lacks improvement
